claim: Prediction in atom ATOM_T002_S012_PRE_01 ("And my main argument is that, you know, machine learning in the future is rea...") holds if and only if the first-principle constraint in atom ATOM_T013_S019_FIR_01 ("fact that it is in principle possible to automate this work. And there should...") is binding.
first_principles_validity_hypothesis: The prediction "And my main argument is that, you know, machine learning ..." is forced by the underlying constraint "fact that it is in principle possible to automate this wo..."; if the constraint is not binding the prediction may still hold by accident but the binding case is the load-bearing version of this candidate.
why_novel_vs_speaker: The speaker introduces the prediction (snippet S012) and the first-principle (snippet S019) in separate contexts; the speaker does not state that the second forces the first.
why_potentially_useful: A prediction that is forced by a first-principle is more falsifiable than a free-floating prediction: if the principle holds and the prediction fails, one of them is wrong, which is a binary research question.
